result:
I will repair this explication step by step.

## Analysis of the error

The draft has a structural problem: referent 2 (the someone who receives enlightenment) is used in the first clause's undergoer role, but it is never formally introduced with a `"bind"` property. Referent 1 (the agent) is frame-implicit in InstanceSchema, so it doesn't need binding; but referents 2, 3, and any others must be introduced by exactly one SP carrying `"bind":n`.

## Repair strategy

The dominant everyday sense of "enlighten" is: **one someone causes another someone to come to know something, typically by giving them information/words**.

I will:
1. Introduce referent 2 (the recipient) with `"bind":2` in an appropriate SP
2. Introduce referent 3 (the topic/thing understood) with `"bind":3` 
3. Simplify the clause structure to be legal and clear
4. Remove the malformed LIKE clause (the comparison doesn't add semantic value and risks further errors)
5. Keep the core: agent DOES something (acts on recipient with words as instrument), BECAUSE of which recipient comes to KNOW the topic

```json
{"kind":"explication","gloss":"one someone causes another to know something by giving them words about it.","record":{"schema":"kot-ast/1","frame":"InstanceSchema","referents":[{"index":1,"refKind":"SomeoneRef"},{"index":2,"refKind":"SomeoneRef"},{"index":3,"refKind":"SomethingRef"}],"clauses":[{"type":"pred","pred":"DO","roles":{"agent":{"kind":"ref","index":1},"undergoer":{"kind":"sp","head":{"kind":"primeHead","prime":"SOMEONE"},"bind":2},"instrument":{"kind":"sp","head":{"kind":"primeHead","prime":"WORDS"},"mods":[{"mod":"GOOD"}]}}},{"type":"op","op":"BECAUSE","args":[{"type":"pred","pred":"DO","roles":{"agent":{"kind":"ref","index":1},"undergoer":{"kind":"ref","index":2},"instrument":{"kind":"sp","head":{"kind":"primeHead","prime":"WORDS"}}}},{"type":"pred","pred":"KNOW","roles":{"experiencer":{"kind":"ref","index":2},"topic":{"kind":"sp","head":{"kind":"primeHead","prime":"SOMETHING~THING"},"bind":3}}}]}]}}
```
